title: Lightning Strikes a Shocking Record
story: Zap! In 2020, a lightning bolt lit up the sky like never before. This incredible flash stretched an amazing 477 miles across Texas, Louisiana, and Mississippi. That's longer than the entire state of California! The World Meteorological Organization confirmed it as the longest single lightning strike ever recorded. Scientists were amazed by this powerful display of nature on April 29.
activity_question: How long was the record-breaking lightning flash?
follow_up_question: Why do you think scientists study extreme weather events like this?

title: Heroic Medic Saves Lives Under Fire
story: Bullets whizzed overhead as Desmond Doss crawled through the battlefield. This brave medic wasn't carrying a weapon. Instead, he was rescuing wounded soldiers one by one. On April 29, 1945, during the fierce Battle of Okinawa, Doss saved 75 of his fellow soldiers at Hacksaw Ridge. He did all this while refusing to carry a gun due to his beliefs. Doss's incredible bravery later inspired an Oscar-winning movie.

activity_choices: He led a charge against the enemy
He cooked meals for the troops

title: Giant Space Rock Zooms Past Earth
story: A massive asteroid hurtled through space, giving Earth a cosmic close call. On April 29, 2020, the mile-wide space rock known as 1998 OR2 zipped by our planet. Though it sounds scary, the asteroid actually passed at a safe distance of 3.9 million miles away. That's over 16 times farther than the Moon! Scientists tracked its journey, reminding us how vast and full of wonders our universe truly is.

title: Rubella Vanishes from the Americas
story: Victory against a virus! In 2015, health experts announced amazing news on April 29. German measles, also called rubella, was officially wiped out in North and South America. This marked the first time an entire world region had beaten this tricky disease. Rubella can cause serious problems for unborn babies, so this achievement meant safer pregnancies for millions of people.

bonus_fact: Vaccines were the key to beating rubella. They teach our bodies how to fight off the virus before we ever catch it!

bonus_fact: Trolleybuses are still used in many cities today, running on electricity without needing tracks like trams do.

title: A Surprising Meeting in the Congo
story: Deep in the heart of Africa, two famous explorers crossed paths. Henry Morton Stanley, known for his daring expeditions, finally found Emin Pasha on April 29, 1888. This chance encounter happened in Old Kavallison, Congo, after Stanley's long journey through dense jungles and treacherous rivers. Their meeting sparked excitement in the world of exploration.

title: A Musical Meeting Changes Everything
story: Sparks flew when Canadian folk singer Joni Anderson performed in Toronto in 1965. On April 29, she met American folk singer Chuck Mitchell backstage. Their connection was instant, leading to marriage and a new name. Joni Anderson would soon become known worldwide as Joni Mitchell, one of the most influential singer-songwriters of her generation.

title: Alan Jackson's Jukebox Hit
story: Country music fans were in for a treat on April 29, 1991. Alan Jackson released his catchy single 'Don't Rock the Jukebox', which quickly became a sensation. title: Elvis Stamp Rocks the Vote
story: Fans couldn't help falling in love with the idea of an Elvis Presley postage stamp. In 1992, the U.S. Postal Service let people vote on which image of the King should grace their letters. April 29 marked the last day to cast ballots in this special election. Millions of Elvis enthusiasts made their voices heard, proving his legacy was still going strong.
activity_question: What did people vote on in 1992?
bonus_fact: The winning Elvis stamp became one of the most popular U.S. stamps ever.
